stem: What is the exponent of the highest power of the prime \( [[p]] \) that divides \( [[N]]! \)? (That is, the largest integer \( k \) such that \( [[p]]^{k} \) divides \( [[N]]! \).)
solution: By Legendre's formula the exponent is \( \sum_{i\ge 1} \lfloor [[N]]/[[p]]^{i} \rfloor = \lfloor [[N]]/[[p]] \rfloor + \lfloor [[N]]/[[p]]^{2} \rfloor + \cdots = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This keeps only the first term \( \lfloor [[N]]/[[p]] \rfloor \), ignoring higher powers.
This truncates the sum after three terms and adds a spurious one.
This is one more than the correct exponent.
This is one less than the correct exponent.
This is two more than the correct exponent.
This is two less than the correct exponent.
This is three more than the correct exponent.

stem: How many trailing zeros does \( [[N]]! \) end in when written in base 10?
solution: Each trailing zero comes from a factor of \( 10 = 2\cdot 5 \), and factors of 2 are far more abundant than factors of 5 in \( [[N]]! \), so the number of trailing zeros equals the exponent of 5 in \( [[N]]! \): \( \lfloor [[N]]/5 \rfloor + \lfloor [[N]]/25 \rfloor + \cdots = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This keeps only the first term of the exponent-of-5 sum, ignoring higher powers of 5.
This counts factors of 2 instead of the scarcer factor of 5, which actually limits the trailing zeros.
This divides by 10 directly instead of summing powers of 5.
This is one more than the correct count.
This is one less than the correct count.
This is two more than the correct count.
This is two less than the correct count.

stem: What is the highest power \( k \) such that \( [[base]]^{k} \) divides \( [[N]]! \), where \( [[base]] = [[p]]^{[[e]]} \cdot [[q]] \)?
solution: Write \( [[base]] = [[p]]^{[[e]]}\cdot[[q]] \). The Legendre exponent of \( [[p]] \) in \( [[N]]! \) is \( [[epx]] \), so \( [[p]]^{[[e]]} \) can appear \( \lfloor [[epx]]/[[e]] \rfloor = [[capp]] \) times; the Legendre exponent of \( [[q]] \) is \( [[eqx]] \). The highest power of \( [[base]] \) dividing \( [[N]]! \) is limited by whichever runs out first: \( \min([[capp]], [[eqx]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses only the exponent of \( [[q]] \), ignoring the constraint from \( [[p]] \).
This uses the raw exponent of \( [[p]] \) without dividing by \( [[e]] \), and ignores \( [[q]] \).
This uses \( \lfloor e_{[[p]]}/[[e]]\rfloor \) alone, ignoring whichever prime is actually the bottleneck.
This is one more than the correct exponent.
This is one less than the correct exponent.
This is two more than the correct exponent.
This is three more than the correct exponent.

stem: What is the exponent of the highest power of the prime \( [[p]] \) that divides the binomial coefficient \( \binom{[[N]]}{[[k]]} \)?
solution: By Legendre's formula, the exponent of \( [[p]] \) in \( [[N]]! \), \( [[k]]! \), and \( ([[N]]-[[k]])! \) are \( [[eN]] \), \( [[ek]] \), and \( [[eNk]] \) respectively. Since \( \binom{[[N]]}{[[k]]} = \dfrac{[[N]]!}{[[k]]!([[N]]-[[k]])!} \), the exponent of \( [[p]] \) in the binomial coefficient is \( [[eN]] - [[ek]] - [[eNk]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses only the exponent from \( [[N]]! \), forgetting to subtract the denominator's contributions.
This uses only the exponent from \( [[k]]! \), an unrelated single term.
This uses only the exponent from \( ([[N]]-[[k]])! \), an unrelated single term.
This subtracts only the \( [[k]]! \) contribution, forgetting the \( ([[N]]-[[k]])! \) term in the denominator.
This is one more than the correct exponent.
This is one less than the correct exponent.
This is two more than the correct exponent.

stem: For the even number \( [[N]] \), the double factorial is \( [[N]]!! = 2\cdot 4\cdot 6\cdots [[N]] \). What is the exponent of the highest power of 2 that divides \( [[N]]!! \)?
solution: Factor a 2 out of each of the \( [[M]] \) even terms: \( [[N]]!! = 2^{[[M]]}\cdot [[M]]! \) where \( [[M]] = [[N]]/2 \). The exponent of 2 in \( [[M]]! \) is, by Legendre's formula, \( [[eM]] \). So the total exponent of 2 in \( [[N]]!! \) is \( [[M]] + [[eM]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses only the exponent of 2 inside \( [[M]]! \), forgetting the \( 2^{[[M]]} \) factored out of the even terms.
This uses only the factored-out power \( 2^{[[M]]} \), forgetting the extra factors of 2 already inside \( [[M]]! \).
This doubles the internal exponent instead of adding the factored-out power once.
This double-counts the internal exponent of 2 in \( [[M]]! \).
This is one more than the correct exponent.
This is one less than the correct exponent.
This is two more than the correct exponent.
